summary: Do not promote. The source helpers are aligned, but the shipped build does not satisfy the discovery contract in substance: `next build` is statically prerendering `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml` (`.next/prerender-manifest.json`, `.next/server/app/robots.txt.body`, `.next/server/app/sitemap.xml.body`). That binds discovery output to build-time env/DB state instead of the runtime `SITE_URL` and runtime published-content boundary. The current `@seo-discovery` coverage only runs against `next dev` via `scripts/playwright-web-server.mjs`, so it misses this production behavior. In a real built deployment, robots can stay fail-closed even when `SITE_URL` is configured at runtime, and the sitemap can snapshot stale build-time content instead of current published notes.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Public home and note pages use the same shared canonical helper in source.
missing_requirements: Exit criterion 1 is not satisfied in substance: the built `/sitemap.xml` is prerendered, so it is not reliably derived from the current runtime public-note set.
Exit criterion 2 is not satisfied in substance: the built `/robots.txt` is prerendered, so runtime `SITE_URL` configuration is not guaranteed to control sitemap advertisement.

The objective to keep discovery output automatically aligned with publish, unpublish, and slug changes is not fully met for deployed builds because discovery is currently optimized as static build output instead of a strict runtime extension of the existing public-content boundary.
Production-style proof for discovery is missing. Current E2E exercises `next dev`, not the built `next start` path where the staticization issue appears.
human_review_triggers: Discovery routes are being shipped as prerendered build artifacts instead of runtime/public-boundary output.
Robots and sitemap can diverge from runtime `SITE_URL` because they are captured at build time.
The verification strategy for this slice does not currently cover built-app discovery behavior, only dev-server behavior.
